description:
To properly set the permissions of /etc/selinux, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0755 /etc/selinux
rationale:
Setting correct permissions on the /etc/selinux directory is important
because this directory hosts SELinux configuration. Protection of this
directory is critical for system security. Restricting the permissions
ensures exclusive control of the SELinux configuration.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /etc/selinux, run the command: 
$ ls -l /etc/selinux
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: 0755
fixtext:
Configure the "/etc/selinux" file to "0755" by running the following command:
$ sudo chmod 0755 /etc/selinux
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/selinux file must have mode 0755 or less permissive to prevent unauthorized access.
title: Verify Permissions On /etc/selinux Directory
